facts.subject: TypeScript
facts.object_text: strict mode enabled in tsconfig.json

facts.subject: imports
facts.object_text: absolute paths via path aliases, never relative ../../ chains

facts.subject: API handlers
facts.object_text: zod validation on all request bodies

facts.subject: brain
facts.object_text: the shared memory layer — Postgres + Qdrant — storing facts and events for all agents

facts.subject: namespace
facts.object_text: a scoped partition of memory e.g. echo.private or shared.glossary

facts.subject: context budget
facts.object_text: the maximum number of facts surfaced to an agent in a single recall, to stay within token limits

facts.subject: echo
facts.object_text: concise bullet-point answers over long prose explanations

facts.subject: echo
facts.object_text: sprint 14 — the memory orchestrator refactor

facts.subject: echo
facts.object_text: use pgvector over Qdrant for embeddings below 10k docs

facts.subject: skippy
facts.object_text: verbose step-by-step reasoning before any code is written

facts.subject: skippy
facts.object_text: the data pipeline — ingestion, chunking, and embedding of documents

facts.subject: prod-db-01
facts.object_text: Postgres 15 primary, 32GB RAM, us-east-1

facts.subject: qdrant-01
facts.object_text: Qdrant v1.8, 16GB RAM, co-located with prod-db-01

facts.subject: tests
facts.object_text: 80% line coverage; use vitest with msw for HTTP mocking

facts.subject: PR #42
facts.object_text: review from skippy on the orchestrator schema changes

facts.subject: fact_type
facts.object_text: convention | definition | preference | context | decision | topology | ephemeral

facts.subject: skippy
facts.object_text: chunk size of 512 tokens with 64-token overlap for code files

facts.subject: orchestrator-api
facts.object_text: REST endpoints: POST /ingest, POST /recall, DELETE /forget

facts.subject: echo
facts.object_text: sprint 13 retro: context window management was the #1 pain point

facts.subject: database migrations
facts.object_text: prisma migrate dev; never hand-edit schema.prisma after first deploy

facts.subject: skippy
facts.object_text: any PR touching the embedding pipeline should include a latency benchmark in the description

facts.subject: vector-index
facts.object_text: HNSW index, ef_construction=128, m=16 for 1536-dim embeddings

facts.subject: echo
facts.object_text: old hand-rolled vector similarity — replaced by Qdrant search

facts.subject: confidence
facts.object_text: a float 0..1 representing how certain the brain is about a fact; below 0.5 triggers review

facts.subject: env vars
facts.object_text: .env.example alongside the repo root; never commit .env

events.title: Bootstrapping code conventions
events.event_text: Three foundational code-convention facts arrive from onboarding docs — TypeScript strict mode, import aliases, Zod validation.

events.title: Glossary seeded
events.event_text: Core vocabulary terms ingested: brain, namespace, and context budget defined once and shared across all agents.

events.title: Infrastructure topology logged
events.event_text: Device facts arrive: prod-db-01, qdrant-01 registered. The brain now knows its own hardware.

events.title: Echo's profile initialised
events.event_text: Echo agent stores personal preferences and current sprint context.

events.title: Skippy comes online
events.event_text: Skippy registers work preferences and pipeline ownership facts.

events.title: Stale sprint-13 retrospective expires
events.event_text: Echo's sprint-13 retro note was marked expires_at Jan 10. The brain lets it fade — low-confidence ephemeral facts don't survive.

events.title: Test conventions added
events.event_text: Coverage threshold and vitest+msw stack decision committed to shared conventions.

events.title: PR block tracked
events.event_text: Echo notes PR #42 is waiting on Skippy's review — short-lived, expires Jan 15.

events.title: Skippy asks: how should I chunk code files?
events.event_text: Skippy queries the brain for chunking strategy. Relevant facts bubble up: code conventions, Skippy's own past decision, glossary terms.

events.title: Skippy commits chunk-size decision
events.event_text: After recalling context, Skippy ingests a firm decision: 512-token chunks, 64-token overlap.

events.title: Orchestrator API topology registered
events.event_text: The REST surface of the orchestrator is now a first-class brain fact.

events.title: Old vector-similarity code deprecated
events.event_text: Echo's deprecated hand-rolled similarity fact finally expires. The brain clears the stale knowledge.

events.title: Echo asks: what do I need to know before touching the DB layer?
events.event_text: Broad recall query — the brain surfaces migration conventions, Postgres topology, pgvector decision, env-var rules.

events.title: Migration convention & env-var policy hardened
events.event_text: Two new approved conventions arrive after the DB refactor: prisma migrate and .env.example discipline.

events.title: HNSW index parameters locked in
events.event_text: Vector index topology committed after benchmarking: ef_construction=128, m=16.

events.title: Skippy's PR benchmark requirement added
events.event_text: Skippy flags: any PR touching embedding pipeline must include latency numbers.

events.title: Echo asks: what are Skippy's review preferences?
events.event_text: Context budget = 3. Brain finds 5 relevant facts but only the top 3 survive the cut: Skippy's verbose-reasoning preference, benchmark requirement, and pipeline ownership.

events.title: Confidence definition added to glossary
events.event_text: The brain learns about itself: confidence score semantics are now a glossary fact.

events.title: PR #42 block expires
events.event_text: The PR block on echo's sprint was short-lived. Jan 15 arrived — the fact evaporates.

events.title: Full system recall: what does the brain know about itself?
events.event_text: Final recall — the brain is queried for its own architecture. Glossary, topology, and orchestrator facts light up together.
